title: hopea iriana
intro: Hopea iriana, also known as the Borneo ironwood tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is a large, deciduous tree native to the tropical rainforests of Borneo, with medicinal properties that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The tree bark, leaves, and roots of Hopea iriana are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, and have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. Hopea iriana can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or compress to treat wounds and skin conditions.
